title: Create a risk war room from a port disruption
given: SCRI has open port-disruption events with affected shipment and supplier matches plus active recommendations.
when: A risk user opens /assistant/risk-war-room and chooses Create war room.
then: The assistant clusters the SCRI events and persists an AssistantRiskWarRoom draft.
The exposure map highlights affected shipments, suppliers, orders, and customers by exposure score.
A Twin scenario draft and risk signal are linked from the primary SCRI event.

title: Queue mitigations for human approval
given: A draft risk war room contains mitigation steps and customer/supplier/internal communication drafts.
when: The risk owner adds an approval note and selects Queue mitigation.
then: An AssistantActionQueueItem is created with risk score, severity, scenario draft, and primary SCRI event context.
The war room moves to MITIGATION_QUEUED.
No SCRI event, Twin scenario run, shipment, supplier, order, or customer message is mutated automatically.

title: Acknowledge risk without closing source events
given: A risk war room has been reviewed by the risk owner.
then: The war room records acknowledgedAt and approvedByUserId.
An assistant audit event records the acknowledgement.
SCRI review state and Twin risk signal acknowledgement remain separate source workflows.
